programs.name: AHCCCS for Elders
programs.what_it_does: Provides health insurance coverage including Medicaid for Arizona residents aged 65 or older who meet income limits, with no monthly premiums for qualified individuals. Covers medical services for those not needing nursing home level of care.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Arizona residents aged 65+ who are U.S. citizens or qualified immigrants, have a Social Security number, and meet income limits (e.g., $1,330/month for single applicant as of 02/01/2026).[4]
programs.benefit_value: No monthly premiums; full health coverage based on eligibility; income limit $1,330/month for one person.[4]

programs.name: Arizona Long Term Care System (ALTCS)
programs.what_it_does: Administers home and community-based services (HCBS) waivers to provide long-term care services in home or community settings as an alternative to nursing homes. Offers services like personal care, homemaker support, and adult day health through contracted managed care organizations.[3][4]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ or disabled individuals meeting nursing facility level of care, income, and asset limits; Arizona residents.[3][4]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by need; covers cost of services like personal care aides, respite, and home modifications to avoid institutionalization.[3][4]

programs.name: Arizona PACE Programs
programs.what_it_does: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE) provides comprehensive medical and social services to eligible frail seniors in community settings, including adult day health, transportation, and home care. Integrates Medicare and Medicaid benefits for coordinated care.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Arizona seniors aged 55+ certified as needing nursing home level care but able to live safely in community; must reside in PACE service area.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Full capitation coverage of all Medicare/Medicaid services plus extras; no cost for full dual eligibles.[3]

programs.name: Arizona Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: Helps low-income Medicare beneficiaries pay for Medicare Part A/B premiums, deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments through Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), and Qualifying Individual (QI) categories. Administered via AHCCCS/ALTCS application process.[4][6]
programs.who_its_for: Medicare enrollees aged 65+ (or disabled) meeting specific federal income/asset limits; higher limits for QI/SLMB than QMB.[4][6]
programs.benefit_value: QMB: Full Part A/B cost coverage (income ~$1,255 single); SLMB: Part B premium (~$185/mo); QI: Part B premium for slightly higher income.[4][6]

programs.name: Nutrition Assistance (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly electronic benefits via EBT card for purchasing eligible food items at approved retailers to supplement diets of low-income seniors. Expedited and senior-specific application processes available through Arizona DES.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Arizona seniors 60+ with income below ~130-200% federal poverty level (varies by household); automatic eligibility for SSI recipients.[1][3]
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per senior household depending on income/size; maximum ~$291 single person (2026 rates).[1][3]

programs.name: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides one-time grants to help eligible households pay heating/cooling bills and address energy crises through Arizona DES and utility partners like APS/SRP. Includes crisis intervention and weather-related assistance.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Arizona households including seniors 60+ at or below 150% federal poverty level; priority for elderly/disabled.[3]
programs.benefit_value: $200-$1,000 one-time grant per year depending on fuel type, income, and crisis level.[3]

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: Funds energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, and furnace repair/replacement for low-income homes to reduce utility costs and improve health/safety. Administered through community action agencies and Area Agencies on Aging partners.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Arizona households (seniors prioritized) at or below 200% poverty; especially elderly, disabled, children.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Free services valued $5,000-$10,000 per home; lifetime energy savings exceed costs.[3]

programs.name: Arizona State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free, unbiased counseling on Medicare benefits, Medicare Advantage, Part D plans, and assistance programs to help seniors select optimal coverage. Provided statewide through Area Agencies on Aging and DES.[3]
programs.who_its_for: All Arizona Medicare beneficiaries, families, caregivers; no income/age restrictions beyond Medicare eligibility.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; average savings $500+/year through better plan choices.[3]

programs.name: Home Delivered Meals (Meals on Wheels)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors through Area Agencies on Aging and providers like FSL to combat malnutrition and isolation. Often includes safety check-ins and nutrition education.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound Arizona seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, meeting income guidelines (often suggested donation).[1][3]
programs.benefit_value: 5-14 meals/week at ~$5-8/meal cost or suggested donation; federal/state funding covers most.[1][3]

programs.name: Arizona Caregiver Respite Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides temporary relief for family caregivers through payment for respite services like in-home care or adult day programs via ALTCS waivers and Area Agency on Aging funds. Supports caregiver health and prevents burnout.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Unpaid family caregivers of Arizona seniors/disabled receiving ALTCS or meeting low-income criteria.[3]
programs.benefit_value: $15-25/hour respite reimbursement up to annual limits based on funding/need.[3]

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time community service work training at minimum wage with host agencies to help unemployed low-income seniors gain skills and transition to unsubsidized employment. Operated through Arizona@Work/American Job Centers.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Arizona residents aged 55+ who are unemployed, income <125% poverty, U.S. citizen/resident.[1]
programs.benefit_value: 20 hours/week at AZ minimum wage (~$13.85/hr in 2026) = ~$1,100/month training wage.[1]

programs.name: Arizona Senior Legal Helpline
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal advice, screening, and referrals for civil legal issues affecting seniors through Area Agencies on Aging and legal aid partners. Covers housing, benefits, consumer issues, abuse prevention.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Arizona residents 60+ with low-moderate income facing civil legal problems.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Free consultations; pro bono/full representation for eligible cases valued $2,000+.[5]

programs.name: Arizona Long Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents of long-term care facilities by investigating complaints, resolving issues, and educating on rights through certified ombudsmen volunteers/staff. Operates statewide via AAA and DHS.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Residents/family members of Arizona nursing homes, assisted living, adult foster care/boarding homes.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy services; resolves 80%+ complaints without litigation; protects against abuse/neglect.[5]

programs.name: Property Tax Refund Credit
programs.what_it_does: Provides a refundable tax credit to eligible seniors to reduce property tax burdens. It helps low-income seniors maintain homeownership by offsetting property taxes paid.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Homeowners aged 65 and older with total household income below specified thresholds.[1]
programs.whats_unique: Arizona-specific state-funded refund credit beyond standard federal programs, targeted at senior homeowners.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by income and property taxes paid; refundable credit up to income-qualified maximums.[1]

programs.name: Senior Property Valuation Protection Program
programs.what_it_does: Freezes property valuations for qualifying seniors to prevent increases in property tax assessments. This stabilizes tax bills over time for long-term homeowners.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ meeting eligibility criteria, typically low-income homeowners.[1]
programs.whats_unique: State-unique program freezing valuations specifically for seniors, not a federal or common state offering.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Tax savings from frozen valuation; amount depends on prevented assessment increases.[1]

programs.name: Arizona Pension Income Deduction
programs.what_it_does: Allows deduction of pension income from state taxable income for retirees. Combined with no tax on Social Security, it reduces overall state tax liability.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors receiving qualifying pension income, including public pensions.[1]
programs.whats_unique: Arizona-specific deduction on pensions beyond federal exemptions; not all states offer equivalent relief.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Deduction up to $2,500 per person on qualifying pension income.[1]
